title: FIFA Launches 'Canada Celebrates' — 38-Stop National Fan Engagement Programme Ahead of World Cup
detail: FIFA announced on April 9, 2026 the launch of 'Canada Celebrates,' a nationwide fan engagement initiative spanning 38 stops across 34 communities in Canada throughout the World Cup window (June 11 – July 19). The programme is designed to extend the tournament's cultural and commercial footprint across all six Canadian time zones, with FIFA stating that more than 75% of Canadian residents will be within a two-hour drive of at least one stop. Canada is one of three co-hosts alongside the United States and Mexico, with two Canadian host cities: Toronto (BMO Field) and Vancouver (BC Place). Canada's national team — qualified for their second consecutive World Cup — is drawn in Group B alongside Qatar, Switzerland, and Bosnia-Herzegovina. The initiative is a significant outreach push to engage Canadian fans beyond the host stadium cities and arrives as the FIFA Congress gathers in Vancouver on April 30 — 22 days before the opening match on June 11. Canada Soccer CEO Nick Bontis had previously described the tournament as a 'transformational moment for football in Canada.' The programme marks a visible escalation in tournament engagement efforts with 63 days to kickoff.

media.caption: FIFA Launches 'Canada Celebrates' — 38-Stop National Fan Engagement Programme Ahead of World Cup

title: US Soccer Deploys Drone Analytics and AI Scouting for World Cup Prep — Pochettino to Name 26-Man Roster May 26 in NYC
detail: New reporting on April 9, 2026 revealed that US Soccer's data science team is deploying drone aerial filming and advanced analytics platforms to support Mauricio Pochettino's World Cup preparation — generating detailed opposition scouting reports and performance metrics from training sessions. Pochettino will formally announce his final 26-player roster on May 26 in New York City, selecting from a pool of approximately 35–40 candidates across European leagues and MLS. The analytics programme represents a significant professionalisation of US Soccer's technical operations; with the USMNT's brutal pre-tournament camp (2–5 Belgium, 0–2 Portugal) fresh in memory, data-driven preparation has intensified. MLS players retain a direct path to the roster with several matchdays remaining before the May 26 deadline — with Toronto FC striker Josh Sargent cited among those in contention. The revelation comes as Pochettino's team rebuilds from a combined 2–7 aggregate against Belgium and Portugal with 63 days to the Group D opener against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on June 12. The next competitive fixture for the USMNT is the May 31 friendly against Senegal — the final match before the roster cutdown date.

media.caption: US Soccer Deploys Drone Analytics and AI Scouting for World Cup Prep — Pochettino to Name 26-Man Roster May 26 in NYC
